notes: Expanded fixed method catalog for SSA Cooking (L2).
Designed for interactive UIs: parameters are form-ready; steps include sensory and safety cues.
IDs are stable. Add new methods instead of renaming existing methodKeys.

restingGuidance: Resting helps juices redistribute; larger cuts need longer rest.
Carryover cooking can raise internal temp by ~5–15°F depending on size and method.

coreSafety.handWash: Wash hands after handling raw meat/eggs/seafood.
coreSafety.crossContamination: Use separate boards/knives for raw proteins and ready-to-eat foods.
coreSafety.chill: Refrigerate perishables within 2 hours (1 hour if >90°F).
coreSafety.reheat: Reheat leftovers until steaming hot; stir soups/sauces while reheating.

cueLexicon.sizzle: Audible sizzle indicates surface moisture evaporating and browning starting.
cueLexicon.fond: Brown bits on pan = flavor base; deglaze with liquid.
cueLexicon.nappe: Sauce coats back of spoon; finger drawn through leaves clean line.
cueLexicon.al_dente: Tender with slight core bite; not mushy.
cueLexicon.steam_release: Food releases naturally from pan/grill once browned.

tips: Dry surface browns better than steaming.

texture: Fork-tender veg; desired spring-back for meats.

tips: Tent loosely with foil to protect crust.

cueNotes: Medium-rare warm red center; medium pink center.

tips: Balance prevents motor strain and uneven cooking.
Carve across grain; keep juices on board.

visual: Grates very hot; hand test 2–3 seconds.

tips: Wait for natural release before flipping.
Move to cooler zone on flare-ups.

ui.help: Hot side + cool side improves control.

tips: Keep coals/burners on one side; food on the other.

visual: Crust forms quickly over hot zone.

ui.help: Wrap to push through stall; butcher paper keeps bark better than foil.
Cover to cook through; uncover to brown.

tips: Too much sauce/toppings can sog the center.

visual: Leopard spotting; cheese melts; rim browned.

ui.help: Classic braise uses partial submersion; stew uses full submersion.

texture: Fork slides in; collagen feels silky when done.

tips: A splash of acid brightens heavy stews.

ui.help: Salt the water to season pasta internally.

tips: Ice bath stops cooking and locks color.
Dry well before sautéing for better browning.

ui.help: Aim below simmer—tiny bubbles, not boiling.

visual: Tiny bubbles at edges; surface trembles.

ui.help: Natural release helps tenderness and prevents starchy spurts.

tips: Scrape bottom to avoid burn warning.
Pat very dry; very hot pan.
Salt cure improves seasoning and texture.

visual: Oil at temp; test piece sizzles steadily.

tips: Oil should shimmer; small breadcrumb sizzles.

ui.help: Overcrowding steams food and reduces browning.

visual: Oven spring early; crust deepens color.

tips: Starter should be bubbly and active.

visual: Ear forms; crust deep and blistered.
Top springs back; tester comes out clean-ish.

tips: Chill relaxes gluten and firms fat.
Chill quickly; remove fat cap for cleaner stock.
Cold butter off heat for shine.
Thin with water/acid; thicken with more oil.
Slash thicker pieces for even cook.

texture: Grain bends but not fully cooked.

tips: Low heat to prevent bottom burn.
Rest lets steam equalize; fluff gently.
Low simmer for clear; hard boil for tonkotsu emulsify.
Tare goes in bowl first for control.
Add tofu and scallions near end.

ui.help: Salt later if you prefer firmer skins; early salting can improve flavor.

defaultSteps.title: Add noodles + sauce; finish with lime

ui.help: Uncovered helps surface dry for crisp skin/sear.

tips: Use kosher salt; avoid oversalting thin cuts.
Balance salt + sugar + acid to taste.
Refrigeration slows fermentation and stabilizes flavor.

safety: Keep chamber cool to prevent melting/unsafe temps.

tips: Cheese benefits from resting days to mellow smoke.
Add hot dairy slowly to avoid scrambling.
Ice bath speeds cooling for safety and texture.
